adminPanel.title: Admin Panel
adminPanel.description: Access the full admin dashboard to manage users, view analytics and predictions.

scan.title: Skin Image Analysis
scan.subtitle: Upload an image or use your live webcam for instant AI skin analysis.
scan.cameraHint: Please allow camera access in your browser settings and try again.
scan.tip: Position the skin area inside the blue frame. Ensure good lighting for better accuracy.
scan.loadingSubtitle: CNN model is processing your image. Please wait.

prediction.title: AI Prediction Result
prediction.heatmapPending: Heatmap will appear here once Flask backend is connected
prediction.disclaimer: This is an AI prediction for awareness only. Please consult a certified dermatologist for proper diagnosis.

diseaseInfo.title: Disease Awareness
diseaseInfo.subtitle: Learn about the 7 skin conditions detected by DermaLens. Click any card to see full details.
diseaseInfo.highWarning: High risk — consult a dermatologist immediately if you notice these symptoms.
diseaseInfo.mediumWarning: Medium risk — monitor regularly and see a doctor if symptoms worsen.
diseaseInfo.disclaimer: Images shown are medical reference illustrations. Always consult a certified dermatologist for proper diagnosis.

melanocyticNevi.name: Melanocytic Nevi
melanocyticNevi.description: Common moles that are usually harmless. They are clusters of pigmented cells that appear on the skin.
melanocyticNevi.visualDesc: Typically appears as a small, evenly pigmented dark spot

melanoma.name: Melanoma
melanoma.description: The most serious type of skin cancer, developing in melanocytes. Early detection is critical.
melanoma.visualDesc: Uneven shape with multiple shades — red, black, brown mixed

basalCell.name: Basal Cell Carcinoma
basalCell.description: Most common skin cancer. Rarely spreads but needs treatment. Often looks like a pearly bump.
basalCell.visualDesc: Shiny, translucent bump with a pearly or waxy appearance

benignKeratosis.name: Benign Keratosis
benignKeratosis.description: Non-cancerous skin growths that commonly appear with age, looking waxy or stuck-on.
benignKeratosis.visualDesc: Rough, waxy or scaly surface — looks stuck on to the skin

dermatofibroma.name: Dermatofibroma
dermatofibroma.description: Benign skin growths that commonly appear on the legs as small firm bumps.
dermatofibroma.visualDesc: Small raised bump — dimples inward when pinched from both sides

vascularLesion.name: Vascular Lesion
vascularLesion.description: Abnormalities of blood vessels visible through the skin surface.
vascularLesion.visualDesc: Bright red or purple — caused by blood vessels near the skin surface

actinicKeratosis.name: Actinic Keratosis
actinicKeratosis.description: Rough, scaly patch caused by years of sun exposure. Considered pre-cancerous.
actinicKeratosis.visualDesc: Dry, rough, scaly patch of skin — typically red, pink or brown

precautions.title: Precautions & Skin Care
precautions.subtitle: Follow these guidelines to maintain healthy skin and prevent skin diseases.
precautions.warningBody: If you notice any rapidly changing skin lesion, immediately consult a certified dermatologist. Early detection saves lives.

checks.title: Regular Skin Checks
tips: Look for new or changing moles
Use the ABCDE rule for moles

hydration.title: Skin Hydration
tips: Drink at least 8 glasses of water
Avoid hot showers — use warm water

doctor.title: When to See a Doctor
tips: Mole changes in size or color
Any skin change that worries you
